policy: Critical root SDK entrypoints must have executable Go examples. The manifest covers representative setup, runner, export/import/resume, and verification paths; non-root packages keep package-local examples and tests.

required_examples.name: ExampleSetup
required_examples.rationale: Shows the SDK setup/defaults entrypoint, logger injection, default warn-level behavior, and runtime identity defaults without reading config files.

required_examples.name: ExampleNewRunner
required_examples.rationale: Shows the root runner facade, context-first event streaming, and run-scoped identity override as the default SDK invocation shape.

required_examples.name: ExampleNewRunRecorder
required_examples.rationale: Shows process export and replay as atomic SDK capabilities without re-running model, tool, sandbox, or business harness work.

required_examples.name: ExampleNewVerificationRecorder
required_examples.rationale: Shows verification as observed evidence over a run export, not as hidden command execution or business release-gate policy.

required_examples.name: ExampleValidateResumePayload
required_examples.rationale: Shows the resume payload schema gate that protects interrupted step boundaries before execution continues.

required_examples.name: Example_graphRun
required_examples.rationale: Keeps the graph-facing root option example executable while graph package APIs remain documented in their own package.
